steps.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil, then salt it generously.
steps.tip: It should taste like the sea — about 1 tbsp of salt.

steps.text: Add 200 g spaghetti and cook 1 minute less than the package says.
steps.tip: Stir once early so nothing sticks.

steps.text: While it cooks: melt 4 tbsp butter in a large pan over medium-low heat.
Add the sliced garlic and chili flakes; cook gently until just golden.
steps.donenessCue: garlic slices pale gold at the edges, not brown
steps.tip: Brown garlic turns bitter — low and slow.

steps.text: Scoop out a mug of pasta water, then drain the pasta.
Toss the pasta in the pan with a splash of pasta water until glossy.
steps.donenessCue: sauce looks silky and clings to the strands

steps.text: Off the heat: stir in the parmesan, parsley, and plenty of black pepper.
Taste, adjust salt, and serve straight away.
Pat the chicken thighs very dry with paper towels.
steps.tip: Dry skin = real browning. Wash hands after handling raw chicken.

steps.text: Season both sides with the salt, pepper, and paprika.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a heavy pan over medium-high until it shimmers.
steps.donenessCue: oil shimmering and flowing easily, just before smoking

steps.text: Lay the thighs in smooth-side down and don't move them for 6 minutes.
steps.donenessCue: deep golden-brown crust on the underside
steps.tip: Moving them early tears the crust. Watch for oil splatter.

steps.text: Flip, add the butter, garlic, and thyme, and cook 6 more minutes.
steps.donenessCue: second side golden, butter foaming but not burning

steps.text: Spoon the foaming butter over the chicken for the last minute.
Check doneness with a thermometer: 74 °C / 165 °F at the thickest part.
steps.donenessCue: juices run clear, no pink at the thickest part — but the thermometer is the real test
steps.tip: Color is a hint, temperature is the answer.

steps.text: Rest the thighs on a plate for 5 minutes before serving.
steps.tip: Resting keeps the juices in the meat.

steps.text: Crack 3 eggs into a bowl and beat until fully blended, with a pinch of salt.
Melt 1 tbsp butter in a nonstick pan over LOW heat.
steps.tip: Low heat is the whole secret.

steps.text: Pour in the eggs and wait about 20 seconds before touching them.
Stir slowly and constantly with a spatula, scraping the bottom.
steps.donenessCue: soft creamy curds forming, still slightly glossy

steps.text: Pull the pan off the heat while the eggs still look a little underdone.
steps.donenessCue: just-set curds with a gentle shine, not dry or browned
steps.tip: They finish cooking off the heat. Eggs should reach 71 °C / 160 °F for safety if that matters for you.

steps.text: Season with pepper and serve immediately on toast.

recipes.title: Crispy Grilled Cheese
ingredients: 70 g cheddar (or your melty cheese), grated

steps.text: Butter one side of each bread slice, edge to edge.
steps.tip: Butter the BREAD, not the pan — even browning.

steps.text: Heat a pan over medium-low. Add one slice butter-side down and pile on the cheese.
Top with the second slice, butter-side up.
Cook about 3 minutes until the underside is deeply golden.
steps.donenessCue: underside evenly golden-brown, cheese starting to melt at the edges

steps.text: Flip carefully and cook about 2 minutes more.
steps.donenessCue: both sides golden, cheese fully melted and stretchy

steps.text: Press gently with the spatula for the last 30 seconds.
Rest for a minute, slice diagonally, and eat while it's stretchy.
